lyrics:
I walked along a misty pier
The water dark, the sky unclear
I almost missed her standing there
Half-turned, with sea-salt in her hair
She moved too close and touched my head
“I’m Nothing,” she turned and said
“I’ve been waiting, longing for you.”
And strangely now I felt it too

I found a face I knew
There was nothing left to do
But dissolve into her force
As if I had a choice
I surrender to her always
With a love that can not stay
There is Me and there is Nothing
And may it always be that way

Then there was Nothing-her voice in the air
She didn’t arrive, she was always there
She pulled me apart with a whisper, a glance
And gave back a soul that had lost its chance
Then there was Nothing-no fear, no disguise
Just love in the dark and light in her eyes
No questions, no reason, no need to explain
Then there was Nothing-and Nothing remains

We woke when dawn begins to breathe
She spoke in truths, she opened me
No promises, no binding thread-
Just moments blooming in my head
She sang a name I used to wear
Then let it vanish in the air
She kissed me once, then stepped away-
As if goodbye was how she’d stay

I found a face I knew
There was nothing left to do
But dissolve into her force
As if I had a choice
I surrender to her always
With a love that can not stay
There is Me and there is Nothing
And may it always be that way

Then there was Nothing-her voice in the air
She didn’t arrive, she was always there
She pulled me apart with a whisper, a glance
And gave back a soul that had lost its chance
Then there was Nothing-no fear, no disguise
Just love in the dark and light in her eyes
No questions, no reason, no need to explain
Then there was Nothing-and Nothing remains

She didn’t stay, but stayed in me
A quiet hum, a memory
And even when she isn’t here-
I know that she is always near

Then there was Nothing-her voice in the air
She didn’t arrive, she was always there
She pulled me apart with a whisper, a glance
And gave back a soul that had lost its chance
Then there was Nothing-no fear, no disguise
Just love in the dark and light in her eyes
No questions, no reason, no need to explain
Then there was Nothing-and Nothing remains

Then there was Nothing…
Then there was Nothing…
Then there was Nothing
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track opens with a spacious and atmospheric texture, built on shimmering, delayed electric guitars that create a melancholic and reflective mood. It builds from a restrained, contemplative verse into an explosive, anthemic chorus where distorted guitars and powerful drums drive the energy forward with a cathartic sense of release. The production balances this dynamic range well, creating a wide, clean sonic space where the earnest lead vocal sits clearly atop the dense, layered rock instrumentation. A defining quality is the powerful contrast between the introspective verses and the immediate emotional impact of the choruses, which feel both vast and intimate.

review.review_text: PCBender has always known how to build a song from the ground up, and 'Then There Was Nothing' earns its seven-minute run time the hard way: by actually going somewhere. The shimmering, delay-soaked intro gives way to a chorus that hits like a wave breaking, distorted guitars and drums landing with the kind of cathartic weight you hear on the better end of classic rock radio. The lyric 'she didn't arrive, she was always there' does real philosophical work without straining for it, and the C major key keeps the whole thing open and unguarded even as the arrangement thickens. A highlight that holds the room.
anchors_used: delay-soaked intro texture from audio impression
distorted chorus build from audio impression
quoted lyric: she didn't arrive she was always there
